lede: A look at November 29 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Jacques Chirac and Anna Faris, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Michael Jackson
events.desc: Michael Jackson releases Thriller, the best-selling music album of all time.

events.title: Atari, Inc.
events.desc: Atari releases Pong, the first commercially successful video game.

events.title: Lyndon B. Johnson
events.desc: U.S. President Lyndon B. Johnson establishes the Warren Commission to investigate the assassination of President John F. Kennedy.

events.title: United Nations General Assembly
events.desc: The United Nations General Assembly approve the United Nations Partition Plan for Palestine.

events.title: Albania
events.desc: Albania is liberated by the Partisans.

events.title: FC Barcelona
events.desc: FC Barcelona is founded by Catalan, Spanish and Englishmen. It later develops into one of Spanish and world football's most iconic and strongest teams.

events.title: Thomas Edison
events.desc: Thomas Edison demonstrates his phonograph for the first time.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Union forces under General Ambrose Burnside successfully defend Knoxville, Tennessee from an attack by Confederate forces under General James Longstreet in the Battle of Fort Sanders during the Siege of Knoxville.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: During the American Revolutionary War, the Battle of Fort Cumberland, Nova Scotia, comes to an end with the arrival of British reinforcements.

events.title: Abbasid Caliphate
events.desc: The Abbasid army under Muhammad ibn Sulayman al-Katib defeats the Qarmatians at the Battle of Hama.

births.name: Jacques Chirac
births.line: French soldier and politician, 22nd President of France

births.name: John Layfield
births.line: American wrestler, football player, and news commentator

deaths.text: George Harrison, English singer-songwriter, guitarist, and music producer
Muhammad al-Jawad, the ninth of the Twelve Imams

observances.text: International Day of Solidarity with the Palestinian People (United Nations)
